steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The student claims that putting a work online increases the number of people who experience it. To support this, we need evidence that online access actually reaches more people — a direct audience-size comparison between online access and in-person visits.

steps.title: Eliminate choices that don't address audience size
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Minneapolis Institute of Art has several works by Siah Armajani.': States how many works by Armajani the museum holds — says nothing about how many people experience any of them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Many people who have been to Minneapolis say that the Minneapolis Institute of Art is worth visiting.': Addresses visitor opinions about the museum, not how many people view a specific work online vs. in person. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Mississippi Delta has been praised by some art critics.': Critical acclaim speaks to quality, not to audience size or the effect of online access. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Each year, more people access the online image of Mississippi Delta than visit the Minneapolis Institute of Art in person.': Directly compares online access count to in-person museum visits, proving online availability expands the work's audience. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
